cascade_form: Option D-style: dase text-emb cos sim two-threshold pair partition; Stage1 CTAS uncertain (left_id,right_id); Stage2 staging-driver JOIN x STYLES_DETAILS twice with AI.IF prompt verbatim; merge confident_pos ∪ bq_returned

calibration.method: AI.GENERATE_BOOL with Q7 pair prompt + thinking_budget=0

baseline.sql:
WITH product_selection AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM fashion_product_images.STYLES_DETAILS styles_details
  WHERE true
    AND price <= 500
)
SELECT
  CONCAT(CAST(p1.id AS STRING), '-', CAST(p2.id AS STRING)) AS id
FROM product_selection p1
JOIN product_selection p2
  ON AI.IF(('''
     You will be given two product descriptions. Do both product descriptions describe products of the same category from the same brand, e.g., both are t-shirts from Adidas?
     
     The first product description is:
     ''', p1.productDisplayName, ' - ', p1.productDescriptors.description.value,
           '''
     The second product description is:
     ''', p2.productDisplayName, ' - ', p2.productDescriptors.description.value
          ),
    connection_id => 'us.connection',
    endpoint => 'gemini-2.5-flash'
  )

cascade.method: Option D-style: dase 89×89 cos sim → two-threshold partition → Stage1 staging pairs → Stage2 verbatim AI.IF on uncertain

stage1_ctas.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE fashion_product_images.q7_uncertain_pairs AS
    SELECT left_id, right_id FROM UNNEST([STRUCT(15783 AS left_id, 27952 AS right_id),STRUCT(15783 AS left_id, 28047 AS right_id),STRUCT(15783 AS left_id, 32138 AS right_id),STRUCT(15783 AS left_id, 15754 AS right_id),STRUCT(15783 AS left_id, 15779 AS right_id),STRUCT(15783 AS left_id, 28011 AS right_id),STRUCT(15783 AS left_id, 34601 AS right_id),STRUCT(15783 AS left_id, 36378 AS right_id),STRUCT(15783 AS left_id, 36385 AS right_id),STRUCT(15783 AS left_id, 47525 AS right_id),STRUCT(15783 AS left_id, 49931 AS right_id),STRUCT(15783 AS left_id, 49939 AS right_id),STRUCT(15783 AS left_id, 50544 AS right_id),STRUCT(15783 AS left_id, 50621 AS right_id),STRUCT(15783 AS left_id, 50988 AS right_id),STRUCT(15783 AS left_id, 54188 AS right_id),STRUCT(16834 AS left_id, 21863 AS right_id),STRUCT(16834 AS left_id, 34430 AS right_id),STRUCT(16834 AS left_id, 39856 AS right_id),STRUCT(16834 AS left_id, 9825 AS right_id),STRUCT(16834 AS left_id, 10689 AS right_id),STRUCT(16834 AS left_id, 23165 AS right_id),STRUCT(16834 AS left_id, 30292 AS right_id),STRUCT(16834 AS left_id, 34011 AS right_id),STRUCT(16834 AS left_id, 34106 AS right_id),STRUCT(16834 AS left_id, 34342 AS right_id),STRUCT(16834 AS left_id, 53126 AS right_id),STRUCT(18217 AS left_id, 18345 AS right_id),STRUCT(18217 AS left_id, 29202 AS right_id),STRUCT(18217 AS left_id, 40015 AS right_id),STRUCT(18217 AS left_id, 9358 AS right_id),STRUCT(18217 AS left_id, 34

stage2_run.sql:
SELECT
  CONCAT(CAST(p1.id AS STRING), '-', CAST(p2.id AS STRING)) AS id
FROM fashion_product_images.q7_uncertain_pairs pairs
JOIN fashion_product_images.STYLES_DETAILS p1 ON p1.id = pairs.left_id
JOIN fashion_product_images.STYLES_DETAILS p2 ON p2.id = pairs.right_id
WHERE AI.IF(('''
     You will be given two product descriptions. Do both product descriptions describe products of the same category from the same brand, e.g., both are t-shirts from Adidas?
     
     The first product description is:
     ''', p1.productDisplayName, ' - ', p1.productDescriptors.description.value,
             '''
     The second product description is:
     ''', p2.productDisplayName, ' - ', p2.productDescriptors.description.value
            ),
    connection_id => 'us.connection',
    endpoint => 'gemini-2.5-flash'
  )
